title: Implement SQLite database layer with sqlx
description: In crates/teamserver: database module using sqlx with SQLite. Write migrations for all tables: ts_agents (full schema from Havoc), ts_listeners, ts_links (pivot relationships), ts_loot, ts_audit_log (new). Implement repository structs with async CRUD methods for each table. Use sqlx::migrate! macro. Connection pool setup. Write integration tests.

title: Embed Python runtime in teamserver via PyO3
description: In crates/teamserver: embed a Python 3.x interpreter using PyO3. Initialize the Python VM at startup. Load .py module files from a configured plugins directory. Expose a Python API module (import red_cell) with bindings for: agent access (get_agent, list_agents), listener access, event subscription (on_agent_checkin, on_command_output), and command registration. Thread-safe GIL management with tokio.

title: Teamserver - Demon Agent Protocol
description: Full implementation of the Demon binary C2 protocol for agent communication. Must maintain backward compatibility with existing compiled Demon agents: 0xDEADBEEF magic, AES-256-CBC per-agent encryption, binary packet format, all command IDs (DEMON_INIT=99, COMMAND_CHECKIN=100, COMMAND_GET_JOB=1, etc). Agent cannot be modified.

title: Implement RBAC system (roles and permissions)
description: In crates/teamserver: role-based access control. Define roles: Admin (full access), Operator (can task agents, manage listeners), Analyst (read-only: view agents, sessions, loot; no tasking). Roles configurable per operator in the profile. Middleware/extractor that checks permissions on each WebSocket command and REST endpoint. Write unit tests covering permission boundaries.

title: Implement Python API bindings for teamserver
description: In crates/teamserver: full Python API surface. RegisterCommand(name, description, callback): adds custom command visible to operators. RegisterCallback(event_type, callback): calls Python on agent events. Agent object: .task(command, args), .info (hostname, user, etc). Listener object: .start(), .stop(). Compatible with existing Havoc Python module conventions where possible. Write example module and tests.

title: Client - UI Components
description: All egui UI panels and widgets: login dialog, main window layout, session table (agent list), session graph (node visualization), command console (interactive per-agent shell), file browser, process list, loot viewer, and multi-operator chat panel.

title: Client - Python API
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the client for operator scripting. Python script manager UI panel, custom command registration from scripts, event callbacks, and access to agent/listener objects from Python. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc client-side Python modules.

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (agent tasking)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands for agent interaction. AgentTask: validate agent exists, check operator RBAC permission, enqueue job in agent's job queue, broadcast AgentTask event to all operators. AgentKill. AgentNote (add note to agent). AgentRemove. All with proper RBAC checks.

title: Teamserver - Authentication & RBAC
description: Operator authentication (SHA3-256 password hashing, session management) and role-based access control system. Operators should have differentiated permissions (e.g. admin, operator, analyst/read-only). This is a new feature beyond what Havoc provides.

title: Implement Demon payload builder
description: In crates/teamserver: wrap the Demon agent compilation pipeline in Rust. Given a listener config and Demon options (sleep, jitter, sleep obfuscation technique, injection settings, output format), invoke MinGW and NASM to compile a Demon binary. Stream build output to operator. Deliver compiled EXE/DLL/shellcode bytes back via WebSocket. Validate compiler toolchain availability at startup.

title: Implement AES-256-CBC crypto for agent communication
description: In crates/common: implement AES-256-CBC encryption/decryption for Demon agent communications. Each agent has a unique 32-byte key and 16-byte IV. Use the aes + cbc crates (RustCrypto). Functions: encrypt_agent_data(key, iv, plaintext) -> Vec<u8>, decrypt_agent_data(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>>. Key generation for new agents. Write unit tests verifying interoperability with known Havoc-produced ciphertext.

title: Set up egui client binary and app skeleton
description: In crates/client: main.rs entry point for red-cell-client. eframe/egui app setup with window configuration (title, size, dark theme). App state struct holding: connection status, operator info, list of agents, listeners, loot, chat messages. Placeholder panels for all UI sections. CLI args: --server <url> for teamserver address.

title: Teamserver - REST API
description: HTTP REST API on the teamserver for automation and external tooling integration. OpenAPI spec, endpoints for agents (list/show/kill/interact), listeners (CRUD), loot/credentials, and jobs. Separate authentication from WebSocket (API keys or JWT). New feature beyond Havoc.

title: Implement screenshot, SOCKS5, and Kerberos commands
description: In crates/teamserver: COMMAND_SCREENSHOT (receive screenshot bytes, save to loot, notify operators), COMMAND_SOCKET/SOCKS5 proxy (relay TCP connections through the agent - requires async bidirectional streaming), COMMAND_KERBEROS (roasting, delegation attacks - parse and format results for operators).

title: Teamserver - Listeners
description: C2 listener implementations: HTTP/HTTPS (profile-configured, custom URIs/headers/user-agents), SMB named pipe listener, and listener lifecycle management (create, start, stop, persist to DB). DNS listener planned as follow-on after HTTP and SMB.

title: Common Crate (shared types, protocol, crypto)
description: Implement the shared Rust crate used by both the teamserver and client. Contains: Demon binary protocol types, AES-256-CBC crypto, JSON WebSocket message types for operator communication, TLS certificate generation, and HCL/YAOTL config parsing. This is a foundational dependency for all other crates.

title: Implement process commands (COMMAND_PROC)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all COMMAND_PROC (0x1010) sub-commands: process list (ps), process kill, process inject, token impersonation for spawn. Parse binary subcommand and parameters. Serialize job for agent. Parse callback output and format for operator display.

title: Implement HTTP route for agent callbacks
description: In crates/teamserver: Axum route(s) for Demon agent HTTP callbacks. POST handler on configurable URIs (from listener profile). Read raw body bytes, validate minimum size, check magic bytes before decryption. Return 404 for invalid requests (operational security). Integrate with listener registry so only routes for active listeners accept traffic. Must handle both HTTP and HTTPS.

title: Implement HCL/YAOTL config parsing in common crate
description: Parse the existing Havoc .yaotl profile format (HCL-based) in Rust. Use the hcl-rs crate. Define strongly-typed Rust structs for the full profile schema: Teamserver (Host, Port, Build), Operators (username/password pairs), Demon defaults (Sleep, Jitter, Injection settings), Listener configs (HTTP, SMB, External). Write unit tests with the existing havoc.yaotl profile.

title: Implement HTTP/HTTPS listener (profile-configured)
description: In crates/teamserver: full HTTP/HTTPS C2 listener. Profile-configurable: custom URIs, HTTP headers (e.g. fake server headers), user-agent filtering, fake 404 for non-agent requests, custom response bodies. HTTPS with auto-generated or profile-specified TLS cert. Bind to configurable host:port. Integrate with Axum router. Support multiple simultaneous HTTP listeners on different ports.

title: Implement session table (agent list panel)
description: In crates/client: egui table panel showing all active agents. Columns: ID, hostname, username, domain, IP, PID, process, arch, elevated, OS, sleep/jitter, last checkin. Sortable columns, color coding (elevated = red highlight). Right-click context menu: interact, kill, add note. Double-click to open command console. Filter/search bar.

title: Implement loot viewer and chat panel
description: In crates/client: loot viewer panel (credentials, files, screenshots - filterable by type/agent/time, download button for files). Multi-operator chat panel (message input + scrollable history with operator names and timestamps, broadcasts via WebSocket chat messages).

title: Teamserver - Python Plugin System
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the teamserver to support operator-side scripting modules. Expose API bindings: RegisterCommand, RegisterCallback, agent/listener access. Load .py modules from a configured directory. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc Python modules where possible.

title: Unit tests for common crate
description: Comprehensive unit tests for crates/common. Protocol: round-trip encoding/decoding of all Demon packet types, edge cases (empty data, max size). Crypto: AES-256-CBC encrypt/decrypt, known-answer tests against Havoc-produced ciphertext. JSON WebSocket messages: serialize/deserialize all event types. Config parsing: test against all three existing .yaotl profile files. Target 90%+ coverage.

title: Implement Demon binary protocol types
description: In crates/common: define all Rust structs and enums for the Demon binary C2 protocol. Packet header: Size(u32), Magic(u32)=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID(u32). All command IDs as enum: DEMON_INIT(99), COMMAND_CHECKIN(100), COMMAND_GET_JOB(1), COMMAND_PROC(0x1010), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL(22), COMMAND_TOKEN(40), COMMAND_KERBEROS(2550), COMMAND_SCREENSHOT(2510), COMMAND_SOCKET(2540), etc. Callback types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT, CALLBACK_ERROR, CALLBACK_FILE. Implement binary serialization/deserialization (byteorder or binrw crate). Write unit tests for round-trip encoding.

title: Implement file browser panel
description: In crates/client: file browser UI for navigating agent file system. Tree view with lazy-load (expand directory triggers ls command). File actions: download, upload (file picker dialog), delete. Show file size, timestamps, permissions. Download progress bar. Integration with loot panel (downloaded files appear in loot).

title: Integration tests: mock Demon agent checkin
description: Integration tests that spin up a real teamserver (in-process, ephemeral SQLite) and simulate a Demon agent. Test flow: start teamserver, connect mock agent over HTTP, send DEMON_INIT packet (properly encrypted with test key), verify AgentNew event emitted, send COMMAND_GET_JOB, enqueue a job via WebSocket, verify agent receives job, send CALLBACK_OUTPUT, verify operator receives result.

title: Implement WebSocket client connection to teamserver
description: In crates/client: async WebSocket client using tokio-tungstenite. Connect to wss://host:port/havoc/. Accept self-signed server certs (same as Havoc behavior). Background tokio task for receiving events and updating shared app state (Arc<Mutex<AppState>>). Sending task for queuing outgoing operator commands. Reconnection with exponential backoff. Connection status indicator in UI.

title: Implement operator event broadcast system
description: In crates/teamserver: event broadcasting infrastructure. Use tokio::sync::broadcast channel to push events to all connected operator WebSocket connections. EventBus struct: subscribe() returns a Receiver, broadcast(event) sends to all. Event types from common crate. Handle slow/disconnected operators (drop lagging receivers). Write unit tests.

title: Implement JSON protocol handler and event dispatcher
description: In crates/client: deserialize incoming JSON WebSocket events (using common crate types) and dispatch to UI state updates. AgentNew: add to agents list. AgentUpdate: update agent fields. AgentResponse: append output to agent console. ListenerNew/Update: update listener list. Chat: append to chat. LootNew: update loot panel. All state updates trigger egui repaint.

title: Teamserver - Command Dispatch
description: Command routing and dispatch system: receive parsed commands from agents, route to appropriate handlers, serialize responses back to agents. Covers all Demon commands: process ops, file system, token manipulation, injection, shell execution, Kerberos, screenshot, SOCKS5 proxy, SMB pivot management.

title: Implement command dispatch router
description: In crates/teamserver: central command dispatch module. Routes incoming parsed Demon commands (by command ID) to appropriate handler functions. CommandDispatcher struct with a registry of handlers. Each handler receives: agent ID, request ID, raw data bytes; returns serialized response bytes to enqueue. Modular design so new commands can be added easily.

title: Teamserver - Operator WebSocket API
description: WebSocket endpoint for operator clients. Handles connections, authentication handshake, event broadcasting to all connected operators, and message routing (create listener, generate payload, execute commands, etc). Must match the JSON-over-WebSocket protocol compatible with the red-cell-client.

title: Implement operator session synchronization on connect
description: In crates/teamserver: when a new operator connects and authenticates, send them the full current state: all active agents (AgentNew events), all running listeners, recent activity log. This brings a newly connected client up to date without requiring a full reconnect or restart. Match Havoc behavior.

title: Implement SMB named pipe listener
description: In crates/teamserver: SMB pivot listener using Windows named pipes. Listen on a configurable named pipe path. Receive Demon binary packets from parent agents acting as SMB pivots. Parse and process identically to HTTP packets. Must run on Linux (use a cross-platform named pipe abstraction or document Linux testing limitations). Persist listener config to DB.

title: E2E tests: mock agent + mock operator client
description: End-to-end tests simulating a full operator session. Spin up teamserver, connect mock operator WebSocket client, authenticate, verify session sync, create a listener, connect mock Demon agent, verify agent appears in operator's session table event, task the agent, verify job delivery and response round-trip. Run in CI on every PR.

title: Implement in-memory agent registry
description: In crates/teamserver: thread-safe in-memory agent registry using DashMap or tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap>. Supports: insert new agent, get agent by ID, list all active agents, update agent fields, mark agent as dead, get/set per-agent AES key+IV, per-agent job queue (VecDeque<Job> behind a Mutex). Sync with DB on changes. Write unit tests for concurrency.

title: Implement Demon packet parser and AES handshake
description: In crates/teamserver: parse incoming Demon binary packets. Read 12-byte header (Size, Magic=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID). For new agents (DEMON_INIT): handle key exchange to establish per-agent AES-256-CBC key+IV, store in agent registry. For existing agents: look up key by AgentID, decrypt payload, return parsed command ID + request ID + data bytes. All using types from common crate.

title: Implement operator audit trail
description: In crates/teamserver: persist all operator actions to ts_audit_log table. Log: operator username, timestamp, action type, agent ID (if applicable), command, parameters, result status. Audit every: agent task, listener create/start/stop, operator connect/disconnect, login attempt (success and failure), loot access. Expose via REST GET /audit with filtering/pagination.

title: Implement listener lifecycle management
description: In crates/teamserver: listener manager for creating, starting, stopping, and deleting listeners. ListenerManager struct holds active listener handles (tokio::task::JoinHandle). Methods: create(config), start(name), stop(name), delete(name), list(). Persist state to DB. Handle errors (port already in use, etc). Expose to WebSocket command handlers and REST API.

title: Testing Infrastructure
description: Full test suite: unit tests for common crate (protocol parsing, crypto, config), unit tests for teamserver core, integration tests that spin up a real teamserver with a mock Demon agent checking in, and E2E tests with mock agent plus mock operator client connecting. Tests run in CI.

title: Implement REST API framework and OpenAPI spec
description: In crates/teamserver: REST API infrastructure. Mount /api/v1/ routes on the Axum router. API key authentication middleware (keys configurable in profile). Define OpenAPI spec (using utoipa crate). Rate limiting. JSON error responses. Versioning strategy. This lays the groundwork for all REST endpoint implementations.

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (listener management)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands over WebSocket for listener management. ListenerNew: validate config, create listener, persist to DB, broadcast ListenerNew event. ListenerStart/Stop: lifecycle management. ListenerDelete. All responses broadcast to all connected operators so multi-operator sessions stay in sync.

title: Implement REST endpoints: loot, audit log, and operators
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints for: GET /loot (list credentials/files/screenshots with filtering), GET /loot/:id (download specific loot item), GET /audit (operator activity log with pagination and filtering by operator/agent/time), GET /operators (list connected operators - Admin only), POST /operators (create new operator - Admin only).

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description: Create the Cargo workspace root Cargo.toml at the repo root. Set up the workspace members: crates/teamserver, crates/client, crates/common. Create placeholder lib.rs/main.rs for each crate. Update .gitignore for Rust build artifacts (target/). Set up rustfmt.toml and clippy.toml with project-wide lint settings. Rust edition 2024.

title: Implement agent checkin handler (DEMON_INIT)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle DEMON_INIT (command 99) - the initial agent registration. Parse agent metadata from checkin payload: hostname, username, domain, IPs, PID, PPID, process name, arch, OS version, elevation status, sleep/jitter config. Create AgentInfo, store in registry and DB, broadcast AgentNew event to all operators. Return success response to agent.

title: Implement TLS certificate generation
description: In crates/common: self-signed TLS certificate generation for the teamserver HTTPS listener and WebSocket server. Use rcgen crate to generate RSA/ECDSA certs at runtime (same approach as Havoc). Also support loading existing PEM certs from profile config. Integrate with rustls/tokio-rustls for use in Axum.

title: Client - Core Infrastructure
description: Core egui client binary: application setup, WebSocket client connection to teamserver (with TLS), operator authentication flow, JSON protocol handler and event processing loop. Foundation for all UI components.

title: Project Setup & Infrastructure
description: Initialize the Cargo workspace, set up directory structure, configure CI/CD pipeline, and establish the foundational project scaffolding for the Red Cell C2 Rust rewrite. This epic must be completed before any other development work begins.

title: Implement JSON WebSocket message types (operator protocol)
description: In crates/common: define all Rust structs (with serde Serialize/Deserialize) for the JSON-over-WebSocket operator protocol. Message structure: { Head: { Event, User, Timestamp }, Body: { SubEvent, Info } }. All event types: InitConnection, Login, ListenerNew, ListenerStart, ListenerStop, AgentNew, AgentUpdate, AgentTask, AgentResponse, Chat, BuildPayload, etc. Mirror the existing Havoc event system. Write unit tests for JSON round-tripping.

title: Implement REST endpoints: agents and listeners
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints. Agents: GET /agents (list all), GET /agents/:id (details), DELETE /agents/:id (kill), POST /agents/:id/task (queue a job). Listeners: GET /listeners, POST /listeners (create), PUT /listeners/:name/start, PUT /listeners/:name/stop, DELETE /listeners/:name. Full RBAC checks on each.

title: Teamserver - Logging & Audit Trail
description: Structured logging throughout the teamserver using the tracing crate. Operator audit trail (who ran which command, on which agent, at what time). Loot/credential logging with search. Session activity recording. All logs persisted and queryable. New feature beyond Havoc.

title: Implement login dialog and authentication flow
description: In crates/client: login dialog UI (egui modal). Fields: server URL, username, password (masked). On submit: send InitConnection/Login WebSocket message with SHA3-256 hashed password. Handle auth success (transition to main UI) and failure (show error, stay on login). Save last-used server URL to local config file.

title: Set up structured logging with tracing crate
description: In crates/teamserver: configure the tracing crate for structured, leveled logging throughout the server. Set up tracing-subscriber with JSON formatter for production and pretty formatter for dev. Log levels configurable via RUST_LOG env var or profile. Instrument all major functions with tracing::instrument. Log to stdout and optionally to rolling file (tracing-appender).

title: Unit tests for teamserver core
description: Unit tests for crates/teamserver core modules. Agent registry: concurrent insert/get/update, job queue operations. Event system: broadcast reaches all subscribers, lagging subscriber is dropped. Auth: correct password accepted, wrong rejected, SHA3-256 hash matches. RBAC: each role correctly allows/denies each permission. DB layer: test migrations run clean, CRUD operations work.

title: Implement DNS C2 listener
description: In crates/teamserver: DNS-based C2 listener. Implement a DNS server (trust-dns-server or hickory-dns) that handles A/TXT/CNAME record queries as C2 communication channel. Profile-configurable domain and record types. Encode/decode Demon packets over DNS. This is a new channel beyond what Havoc currently provides. Lower priority - implement after HTTP and SMB are stable.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on every push/PR: cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo fmt --check. Also add a release workflow that builds Linux x86_64 binaries for red-cell and red-cell-client. Cache cargo registry and target dir.

title: Set up Axum server binary and config loading
description: In crates/teamserver: main.rs entry point. Parse CLI args (clap): --profile <path>, --debug. Load and validate the HCL profile (from common crate). Initialize the Axum router with placeholders for all route groups (WebSocket /havoc/, HTTP agent listener, REST API /api/v1/). Bind to configured host:port with TLS (rustls). Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.

title: Implement operator WebSocket handler and connection manager
description: In crates/teamserver: WebSocket endpoint at /havoc/. Upgrade HTTP connection to WebSocket using axum::extract::ws. Per-connection task: receive messages, parse JSON, dispatch to command handlers; subscribe to EventBus and forward broadcasts to client. Connection registry (track all active operator connections). Handle disconnects gracefully. Authentication check on first message before allowing further interaction.

title: Implement process list panel
description: In crates/client: process list UI. Tabular view: PID, PPID, name, arch, user, session. Refresh button. Actions: kill process, inject into process (opens injection dialog), migrate. Filter/search by name or PID. Highlight current agent process.

title: Teamserver - Payload Builder
description: Rust-based payload generation system for producing Demon agent binaries (EXE, DLL, shellcode). Wraps the existing MinGW/NASM compilation pipeline, applies profile-based configuration (sleep, jitter, injection settings, C2 listener endpoints), and delivers output to operators.

title: Implement loot logging and credential capture
description: In crates/teamserver: loot capture and management. Automatically detect and store: credentials in command output (pattern matching), downloaded files (via CALLBACK_FILE), screenshots (via COMMAND_SCREENSHOT). Persist to ts_loot table with metadata (type, agent, operator, timestamp, content/path). Broadcast LootNew event to operators.

title: Implement command console (per-agent interactive shell)
description: In crates/client: interactive command console panel per agent. Input field with command history (up/down arrow). Output area with scrollback (formatted text, color for errors vs output). Tab completion for Demon commands. Display metadata header (agent hostname/user/pid). Multiple tabs or split view for multiple agents. Timestamps on output lines.

title: Embed Python runtime in client via PyO3
description: In crates/client: embed Python interpreter using PyO3. Load .py scripts from a configured scripts directory. Expose a client-side Python API: red_cell.register_command(name, callback), red_cell.on_agent_checkin(callback), red_cell.agent(id) returning an agent proxy object. Python runs in a dedicated thread with GIL. Error isolation (bad script doesn't crash client).

title: Implement job queue and COMMAND_GET_JOB handler
description: In crates/teamserver: handle COMMAND_GET_JOB (command 1) - agent polling for work. Dequeue all pending jobs from the agent's job queue. Serialize jobs into the Demon binary response format (command ID + params per job). If no jobs, return empty response. Handle COMMAND_CHECKIN (100) heartbeat: update agent LastCallIn timestamp in registry and DB, broadcast AgentUpdate event to operators.

title: Implement operator authentication
description: In crates/teamserver: operator login flow. Operators defined in HCL profile (username + plaintext password, hashed at load time with SHA3-256 to match Havoc). WebSocket handshake: client sends {User, Password_SHA3}. Server validates against profile. On success: issue session token (UUID or JWT), associate with WebSocket connection. Reject unknown users or wrong passwords. Write unit tests.

title: Implement token, injection, and shell commands
description: In crates/teamserver: handlers for COMMAND_TOKEN (steal/impersonate/revert), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL (inject DLL into remote process with shellcode), shell command execution, run (execute with output capture), and spawn (new process with token). Serialize jobs correctly per Demon binary protocol.

title: Implement Python script manager UI
description: In crates/client: script manager panel. List loaded .py scripts with status (loaded/error). Load/unload/reload buttons. Script output console (stdout/stderr from scripts). File picker to load a new script. Error display for import failures. Status indicator showing how many commands each script registered.

title: Define shared error types and agent/listener structs
description: In crates/common: define the shared domain types used across teamserver and client. AgentInfo struct (all fields from TS_Agents DB schema: AgentID, Hostname, Username, DomainName, IPs, PID, ProcessName, Arch, Elevated, OSVersion, SleepDelay, Jitter, FirstCallIn, LastCallIn, etc). ListenerConfig enum (Http, Smb, External) with full config fields. OperatorInfo. Custom error types using thiserror.

title: Implement session graph (node visualization)
description: In crates/client: egui node graph visualization of agent topology. Nodes represent agents, edges represent pivot relationships (SMB links). Drag-to-pan, scroll-to-zoom. Node color by status (active/dead). Click node to open agent console. Use egui_graphs crate or implement custom with egui Painter. Show teamserver as root node.

title: Implement pivot management (SMB links)
description: In crates/teamserver: manage agent pivot relationships. When an agent reports an SMB pivot link, store parent/child relationship in ts_links table and registry. Route jobs for child agents through parent agent's job queue (encapsulated in pivot packet format). Handle pivot chain teardown when parent agent dies.

title: Implement agent response handler (CALLBACK_*)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all agent callback response types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT (command stdout/result), CALLBACK_ERROR (error message), CALLBACK_FILE (file download chunk with offset/total). Match response to original request ID. Format output and broadcast AgentResponse event to operators. Handle multi-chunk file downloads (reassemble). Persist output to DB for history.

title: Implement file system commands
description: In crates/teamserver: file system command handlers. Upload (send file to agent in chunks), Download (receive file from agent via CALLBACK_FILE, reassemble, persist to loot), directory listing, file delete, file copy, directory navigation. Track download progress and broadcast to operators.

title: Teamserver - Core Infrastructure
description: Core teamserver infrastructure: Axum HTTP server setup, SQLite database layer (sqlx), in-memory agent registry, and event broadcast system. This is the backbone that all other teamserver components build upon.
